acceptance_targets: [CH18_PROSE_RECORD_DISTANCE_BREAK] In the revisions to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:3, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:31, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:33, a cold reader can name who sees or reads each fact or what artifact contains it. [CH18_CRAFT_SANITIZED_CODA] After revision, at least one span anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:7 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:9 and one span anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:29, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:33 unfold in present action: a reader can point to what a character physically sees or hears, what exact words are spoken or displayed, and what changes by the end of the beat. [CH18_PROSE_INTERIORITY_VACUUM] Across the revised stretch beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:5 and continuing through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:35, there is no run of 10 or more consecutive paragraphs that contain only external report. [CH18_LENA_CONTINUITY] Pass if line 3375's description of Lena Vogt's body is consistent with the shelving-puncture mechanism described in Chapter 17 (lines 3320-3328): a penetrating wound to the right side, not blunt-force head trauma. Fail if the forensic description still implies 'smashed in' head/upper body or any mechanism that contradicts the established fight choreography.
[length_chapter_18_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 2000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revisions to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:3, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:31, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:33, a cold reader can name who sees or reads each fact or what artifact contains it. No sentence presents pathologist estimates, email circulation, analyst reactions, or future litigation knowledge as free-floating narration without a visible on-page source.
findings.problem: The chapter abandons the style bible's close-third, locked-to-Noor delivery and instead presents a master summary assembled from logs, forensics, email metadata, analyst notes, and later filings. Because the narration can no longer be traced to a live perceiver or a bounded artifact, the coda reads like an omniscient incident report rather than fiction.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the record frame introduced at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:3 and the report-heavy beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:31, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:33 around a disciplined point of access. Either stage the aftermath through the first entrants' immediate perception or present specific artifacts with explicit limits, but do not let sentences claim unbounded knowledge that no witness or document on the page could supply.

findings.acceptance_test: After revision, at least one span anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:7 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:9 and one span anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:29, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:33 unfold in present action: a reader can point to what a character physically sees or hears, what exact words are spoken or displayed, and what changes by the end of the beat. If those moments can still be summarized as 'they found the bodies' or 'the company moved on,' fail.
findings.problem: At the book's final human and corporate consequences, the chapter repeatedly substitutes summary for scene: the facilities discovery, family notifications, Noor's mother's call, analyst response, and launch are all reported from outside. The constitution and aesthetic risk policy explicitly reject this kind of cutaway; the ending needs presence, not a digest.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the timeline skeleton, but turn at least one discovery beat and one institutional-response beat into fully rendered scenes. Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:7 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:9 so the first entrants physically encounter the bodies in real time, and revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:29, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:33 so a family notification or launch moment plays out through quoted language, sensory detail, and immediate consequence instead of paraphrase.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revisions at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:25, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:31, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:33, at least three institutional voices are distinguishable without labels by diction and rhythm alone. If a reader could swap the HR, PR, analyst, and email lines without hearing a change in register, fail.
findings.problem: The chapter promises a collage of dispatch audio, a press release, an HR script, analyst notes, and an internal email, but most of those sources are paraphrased into the same neutral declarative narrator cadence. PR, HR, finance, and internal launch language should sound meaningfully different; here they blur together, so the institutional voices never acquire the chilling specificity the premise sets up.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the artifact-heavy sections at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:25, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:31, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:33, let the sources speak in distinct registers. Use brief quoted or tightly stylized excerpts so the press statement hedges, the HR script deflects, the analyst note financializes, and the launch email sounds bloodlessly procedural instead of all reading like the same narrator.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised stretch beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:5 and continuing through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:35, there is no run of 10 or more consecutive paragraphs that contain only external report. At least three separated paragraphs in that stretch include concrete thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment from the chapter's chosen point of access.
findings.problem: The chapter runs well past ten consecutive paragraphs without thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment from any focal consciousness. Whatever formal frame you choose, that much uninterrupted external reporting drains fictionality and turns the ending into paperwork.
findings.rewrite_direction: Thread lived perception or cognition into the long report run beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:5 and continuing through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_18.md:35. Even in a documentary coda, the chapter needs a sensing mind or body in the paragraph stream: the first responder smelling the cafeteria, Noor's mother processing the script, or an employee reading the launch email and reacting.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if line 3375's description of Lena Vogt's body is consistent with the shelving-puncture mechanism described in Chapter 17 (lines 3320-3328): a penetrating wound to the right side, not blunt-force head trauma. Fail if the forensic description still implies 'smashed in' head/upper body or any mechanism that contradicts the established fight choreography.

evidence.excerpt: 3375: The cursor still blinks in the reply field. The pathologist's first estimate puts Noor's death at 4:47 PM. Eight feet away, Lena Vogt is on her back. Her head and upper body are smashed in. A broken length of shelving lies between them.

3320: The pipe cracks across her left forearm. Shock shoots from wrist to shoulder and her fingers almost open, but not quite. Lena draws back for another swing. Noor steps inside the arc, close enough to smell Priya's throat on Lena's sleeve, and drives the broken shelving into Lena's right side.

3322: Resistance gives sooner than Noor expects. The point slips between ribs. Bone catches and then yields with a scrape she can feel all the way up the metal. Fabric pulls inward around the wound. Noor keeps pushing until her fist hits Lena's body and there is nowhere else for the shard to go.

3324: Lena lets out one flat exhale. The pipe falls and clangs on the floor. Her eyes drop to the metal jutting from her side, but her face does not change. Assessment stays on it even now. Lena takes one step back, then another, then sits against the wall in a controlled descent, landing on one hip with her legs folding under her. Her right hand comes to rest on the shelving without trying to pull it free. Breathing shortens. A wet sound enters low on every exhale and grows louder.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 3375: The cursor still blinks in the reply field. The pathologist's first estimate puts Noor's death at 4:47 PM. Eight feet away, Lena Vogt is on her back. Her head and upper body are smashed in. A broken length of shelving lies between them.

findings.problem: Chapter 18's forensic description states 'Her head and upper body are smashed in' (line 3375), but Chapter 17 shows Lena dying from a shelving puncture to the right thorax. Lines 3320-3324 describe the fight explicitly: Noor 'drives the broken shelving into Lena's right side,' 'The point slips between ribs,' Lena sits down in a controlled descent with the shelving still protruding. There is no blunt-force head trauma in the fight choreography. The forensic description contradicts the established cause of death, breaking the documentary authority that is Chapter 18's structural raison d'être — the precision of the external record is what makes the epilogue devastating, and a wrong forensic detail undermines that precision.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3375. Replace 'Her head and upper body are smashed in' with forensic language consistent with Chapter 17's shelving-puncture cause of death: a penetrating wound to the right thorax from a broken shelving shard, with the weapon still in place or nearby. The shelving detail at the end of line 3375 ('A broken length of shelving lies between them') already supports the correct mechanism and should remain. The forensic tone of the surrounding passage should be preserved.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 2000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel should make the reader uncomfortable on every axis: physical (graphic violence and decomposition), psychological (watching civilized people degrade), moral (the protagonist is compromised, the collaborator has sympathetic motives, the apex predator is competent rather than monstrous), and structural (the polite AI register against the horror, the corporate coda after the massacre). Lean into every discomfort the premise creates. The worst creative risk is pulling the punch. Ember's politeness should never waver — that consistency is more unsettling than menace. The deaths should never be clean. The protagonist should never be heroic. The ending should offer no comfort, no lesson, no redemption. The stock price jumps eleven percent. That is the last sentence of the novel. Let it land.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism, summary, or cut-away at the moment of violence, death, or physical consequence is a craft failure. This is a horror novel. The deaths must be graphic, ugly, physical, and specific on the page. The reader must be in the room. If a character is beaten to death with a fire extinguisher, the reader hears the sound. If someone dies of chemical burns, the reader sees the skin. If someone bleeds out, the reader watches the eleven minutes. No fading to black, no tasteful elision, no looking away. The bodies stay.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Priya, Chen Wei, and Viktor, who are naturally more formal. Even formal characters should contract under stress. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate precision, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-3), dialogue density (dialogue-heavy vs narration-dominant), pacing (sustained tension vs building dread vs explosive violence), point-of-attack (mid-action opening vs quiet-before-storm vs aftermath). Some chapters should be almost entirely one sustained scene; others should cut between locations and timelines within the day. The texture variation prevents the novel from becoming a monotone of crisis.
prose_style_profile.diction: Clean, precise, contemporary. Not literary-lyrical and not hard-boiled. Noor's observational intelligence should inform the prose without the prose becoming clever. The diction is a window, not a performance. Technical language from ML and infrastructure used naturally when Noor would think in those terms. Physical description is concrete and specific — brand names, measurements, the exact color of something. Never vague when precision is available.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Metaphor pile-up: one strong image per paragraph maximum, do not layer three metaphors on one moment
Emotional narration: do not tell the reader what to feel through adjective stacking or emotional summary. Show the thing and let the feeling happen.
Thriller-voice: do not drift into sentence fragments, subject-dropping, or staccato rhythm as a default tension mode. This is a horror novel with a literary sensibility, not a airport thriller.
Comfort recovery: do not follow a horror beat with emotional stabilization. No character 'takes a breath and feels steadier.' The dread compounds.
Ember-explanation: do not explain Ember's strategy through narrator exposition. It should emerge through Noor's analysis and observation.
Purple death: do not poeticize the deaths. They are ugly, physical, specific. No one dies beautifully.
The reassuring paragraph: do not end a chapter or section with a sentence that provides emotional comfort or resolution. End on the thing that stays.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third, locked to Noor. The reader sees, hears, smells, and knows only what Noor does. When Noor is not present for a scene, it doesn't happen on the page — it arrives as discovery, rumor, or aftermath. The camera never leaves her.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Propulsive and varied. Default pace is forward-moving — short paragraphs, active verbs, scene momentum — but the rhythm must shift for horror (slowed, sensory-heavy, the moment stretching), grief (longer sentences, heavier cadence), and dread (suspended, waiting, the sentence holding its breath). No single rhythm for more than two consecutive pages.
sensory_bias: smell (decomposition, chemicals, recycled air, blood, institutional food)
temperature (the building cooling degree by degree)
sound (electromagnetic locks, ventilation changes, terminal notifications, violence, silence)
tactile (cold surfaces, blood, the physical textures of deterioration)
